question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens with a valid account ID, causing the DB connection pool in BaseService.pool() to be exhausted and degrading 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a long EVM address string as the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to execute an expensive DB lookup via getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() on every request, and by repeating this at scale degrade 30%+ of mirror node query throughput?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account alias string as :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress, forcing EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias() to execute a full DB scan via entityFromAliasQuery on every request, and by repeating this at scale exhaust DB CPU and degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with both spender.id lower and upper bounds plus token.id lower and upper bounds, causing extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce a three-part UNION ALL query in TokenAllowanceService.getQuery(), and by sending many such requests simultaneously exhaust DB CPU and degrade 30%+ of mirror node nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to the maximum allowed value in extractTokenMultiUnionQuery(), causing TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() to fetch and map the maximum number of rows per request, and by repeating this at scale exhaust server memory and degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with the maximum allowed number of repeated spender.id query parameters (up to config.query.maxRepeatedQueryParameters), causing buildFilters() to generate a large filter array and Bound.parse() to throw InvalidArgumentError on the second occurrence, and by repeating this at scale cause excessive error-handling overhead degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a maximally long alias string as :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress that passes AccountAlias.isValid() but does not match any entity, causing EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias() to execute a full DB query and throw NotFoundError on every request, and by repeating this at scale degrade 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a valid EVM address that does not exist in the entity table, causing EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to execute entityFromEvmAddressQuery on every request and return no rows, and by repeating this at scale exhaust DB connections and degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with spender.id=gte:0 and token.id=gte:0 combined with the maximum limit, causing getSubQuery() to build a query with no upper bound that scans the entire token_allowance table, and by repeating this at scale cause full table scans that degrade 30%+ of mirror node DB nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with order=desc and maximum limit, causing TokenAllowanceService.getQuery() to build a descending ORDER BY query over spender and token_id columns that forces a full index scan, and by repeating this at scale degrade 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link returned by getPaginationLink() to chain an unbounded sequence of paginated requests, each triggering a new DB query via TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances(), and by automating this chain at scale exhaust DB connections and degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both spender.id=gte:X and spender.id=lte:Y with token.id=gte:A and token.id=lte:B, causing extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce lower, inner, and upper subqueries that are combined with UNION ALL in getQuery(), and by sending many such requests simultaneously cause excessive DB query planning overhead degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a valid account ID that has a very large number of token allowances, causing TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() to return the maximum rows and tokenAllowances.map() to construct a large array of TokenAllowanceViewModel objects, exhausting server heap memory and degrading 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of concurrent requests each with a different valid EVM address, causing EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to issue concurrent DB queries via entityFromEvmAddressQuery, exhausting the DB connection pool and degrading 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a maximally large numeric account ID string as :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress, causing EntityId.parseString() to process the value and EntityService.getEncodedId() to return an encoded ID, and by repeating this at scale with IDs that have no allowances cause repeated empty DB scans degrading 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with spender.id=gt:X and token.id=gte:A, causing validateLowerBounds() to throw InvalidArgumentError because primary lower uses gt instead of gte, and by repeating this at scale cause excessive error-handling overhead degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a shard.realm.num formatted account ID where the account has a very large number of token allowances, causing getSubQuery() to build a query with no filter conditions beyond owner and amount, resulting in a full owner-scoped table scan that degrades 30%+ of mirror node DB nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an EVM address that is a valid 20-byte hex string but maps to no entity, causing EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to execute entityFromEvmAddressQuery and return empty rows, and by sending thousands of such requests simultaneously exhaust DB connections and degrade 30%+ of nodes?
